description: Random score F1-tol baseline (5 seeds, threshold=anomaly_rate percentile)
conclusion: Random scores achieve 67.6% F1-tol on SMD vs A2P's 52.07% (random +15.5pp). On SVDB4: random 68.1% vs A2P 67.55% (effectively tied). F1-tol measures segment coverage, not prediction quality.

SVDB1.note: High variance (7.64%) because only 5 short segments; random predictor sometimes hits all, sometimes misses. Still 3x A2P on mean.

summary: Random scores beat A2P on ALL THREE datasets: SVDB4 (68.1% vs 67.55%), SMD (67.6% vs 52.07%), SVDB1 (58.9% vs 19.2%). F1-tol metric is trivially gamed by random noise on datasets with short segments and ±50 tolerance.
